Name: Dal Bhat
Description: Nepal's national dish: steamed rice, lentil soup, vegetable curry & pickles. Eaten twice daily by most Nepalis.

Name: Momo
Description: Steamed or fried dumplings filled with buff, chicken or veg; served with spicy achar sauce.

Name: Thukpa
Description: Hearty noodle soup with vegetables or meat in a clear or spicy broth; popular at high altitude.

Name: Samay Baji
Description: Ritual platter of beaten rice, black soybean, spiced potato, ginger, egg, buff & local wine.

Name: Yomari
Description: Rice flour dumplings filled with molasses & sesame; made during Yomari Punhi festival.

Name: Juju Dhau
Description: Famous 'King Curd' set in clay pots; creamy, slightly sweet; a Bhaktapur pride.

Name: Sel Roti
Description: Crispy ring-shaped sweet rice bread fried in oil; made during Tihar & Dashain festivals.

Name: Thakali Thali
Description: Multi-dish meal with rice, lentil, ghee, meat curry & pickle; famous for generous use of ghee.

Name: Dhido
Description: Thick porridge from buckwheat or millet flour; traditional alternative to rice in hilly regions.

Name: Gundruk
Description: Fermented dried leafy greens; used in soup or as a side dish; Nepal's iconic preserved vegetable.

Name: Chhurpi
Description: Hard dried yak cheese; comes soft or rock-hard; chewed for hours as a snack at high altitude.

Name: Butter Tea (Po Cha)
Description: Tea churned with yak butter and salt; warming and high-calorie drink for cold mountain climates.

Name: Tongba
Description: Traditional millet-based warm alcoholic drink served in a bamboo vessel; sipped through a straw.

Name: Kwati
Description: Protein-rich soup of 9 types of sprouted beans; prepared during Gunla festival.

Name: Chatamari
Description: 'Newari pizza' — thin crispy rice flour crepe topped with egg, minced meat or vegetables.

Name: Aloo Tama
Description: Sour & spicy bamboo shoot curry cooked with black-eyed peas and potatoes.

Name: Bara
Description: Savoury lentil pancake; plain or topped with egg or minced meat.

Name: Choila
Description: Spiced charcoal-grilled buffalo meat marinated in mustard oil; essential in Samay Baji platter.

Name: Mustang Apple Products
Description: Fresh apples, cider, brandy and jam from high-altitude orchards of Mustang valley.

Name: Ilam Tea
Description: Premium orthodox first-flush tea; Darjeeling-style green and black varieties from Nepal's misty hills.
